title: Authors and Citation
text: Hodge D (2026). ggscribe: Annotate 'ggplot2' Visualisation. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/davidhodge931/ggscribe.
code: @Manual{,   title = {ggscribe: Annotate 'ggplot2' Visualisation},   author = {David Hodge},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/davidhodge931/ggscribe}, }

title: Annotate ggplot2 Visualisation
text: objective ggscribe provide helpers annotate ‘ggplot2’ Visualisation. Note: avoid namespace collisions, recommended load package, instead refer function package name (e.g. ggscribe::sec_axis(). sec_axis adjusts space plot, whereas * functions . axis_ticks, axis_text axis_bracket require (1) globally set theme explicit panel dimensions (2) coord_cartesian(clip = "") panel_shade must geoms. reference_line geoms. require annotation text along axis different angles etc, use combination sec_axis axis_* functions. sec_axis function include annotation requires maximum space want plot adjust .

title: Annotate ggplot2 Visualisation
text: Install CRAN, development version GitHub.

title: Annotate ggplot2 Visualisation
text: ggscribe provides various axis panel annotation helper functions.  function ensure text easily coloured contrast fill aesthetic.
code: library(ggplot2) library(dplyr)  set_theme(   ggrefine::theme_grey(     panel_heights = rep(unit(50, "mm"), 100),     panel_widths = rep(unit(75, "mm"), 100),   ) )  mtcars |>   ggplot(aes(x = wt, y = mpg, colour = as.factor(gear), fill = as.factor(gear))) +   scale_colour_discrete(palette = blends::multiply(get_theme()$palette.colour.discrete)) +   #clip = "off" is required for axis_text, axis_ticks and axis_bracket    coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +   #reference lines and shade   ggscribe::reference_line(xintercept = 2.4) +   ggscribe::reference_line(yintercept = 12)  +   ggscribe::panel_shade(     xmin = 4,     xmax = 5,   ) +   #top axis   scale_x_continuous(     sec.axis = ggscribe::sec_axis(       breaks = c(mean(c(4, 5))),       labels = c("Range"),       guide = ggscribe::guide_sec_axis(         angle = 90,       )     )   ) +   ggscribe::axis_bracket(     position = "top",     breaks = c(4, 5),   ) +   ggscribe::axis_text(     position = "top",     breaks = c(2.4),     labels = c("Threshold"),   ) +   #right axis   ggscribe::axis_text(     position = "right",     breaks = 12,     labels = "Threshold",   ) +   #geom   geom_point() +   #annotations fit plot   theme(plot.background = element_rect(colour = "grey92")) ggwidth::set_equiwidth(equiwidth = 1.75)  mtcars |>   count(cyl, am) |>   mutate(     am = if_else(am == 0, "Automatic", "Manual"),     cyl = as.factor(cyl)   ) |>   ggplot(aes(x = am, y = n, colour = cyl, fill = cyl, label = n)) +   geom_col(     position = position_dodge2(preserve = "single", padding = 0.05),     width = ggwidth::get_width(n = 2, n_dodge = 3),   ) +   scale_fill_discrete(palette = jumble::jumble) +   scale_colour_discrete(palette = blends::multiply(jumble::jumble)) +   geom_text(     mapping = ggscribe::aes_contrast(), # or aes(!!!ggscribe::aes_contrast()),     position = position_dodge2(       width = ggwidth::get_width(n = 2, n_dodge = 3),       padding = 0.05,       preserve = "single"),     vjust = 1.33,     show.legend = FALSE,   ) +   scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(c(0, 0.05))) +   ggrefine::hybrid(x_type = "discrete")

what: A mapped aesthetic for text colour on fill — aes_contrast
title: A mapped aesthetic for text colour on fill — aes_contrast
text: Modifies mapped colour (fill) aesthetic contrast fill (colour) aesthetic. Function can spliced ggplot2::aes rlang::!!!.

title: A mapped aesthetic for text colour on fill — aes_contrast
code: aes_contrast(..., dark = NULL, light = NULL, aesthetic = "colour")

title: A mapped aesthetic for text colour on fill — aes_contrast
text: ... Require named arguments (support trailing commas). dark dark colour. NULL, derived theme text panel background. light light colour. NULL, derived theme text panel background. aesthetic aesthetic modified contrast. Either "colour" (default) "fill".

title: A mapped aesthetic for text colour on fill — aes_contrast
text: ggplot2 aesthetic ggplot2::aes.

title: A mapped aesthetic for text colour on fill — aes_contrast
code: library(ggplot2) #>  #> Attaching package: ‘ggplot2’ #> The following object is masked from ‘package:ggscribe’: #>  #>     sec_axis library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: ‘dplyr’ #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:stats’: #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:base’: #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union library(stringr)  set_theme(  ggrefine::theme_light(     panel_heights = rep(unit(50, "mm"), 100),     panel_widths = rep(unit(75, "mm"), 100),  ) )  ggwidth::set_equiwidth(equiwidth = 1.75)  mtcars |>   count(cyl, am) |>   mutate(     am = if_else(am == 0, "Automatic", "Manual"),     cyl = as.factor(cyl)   ) |>   ggplot(aes(x = am, y = n, colour = cyl, fill = cyl, label = n)) +   geom_col(     position = position_dodge2(preserve = "single", padding = 0.05),     width = ggwidth::get_width(n = 2, n_dodge = 3),   ) +   scale_fill_discrete(palette = jumble::jumble) +   scale_colour_discrete(palette = blends::multiply(jumble::jumble)) +   geom_text(     mapping = ggscribe::aes_contrast(), # or aes(!!!ggscribe::aes_contrast()),     position = position_dodge2(       width = ggwidth::get_width(n = 2, n_dodge = 3),       padding = 0.05,       preserve = "single"),     vjust = 1.33,     show.legend = FALSE,   ) +   scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(c(0, 0.05))) +   ggrefine::modern(x_type = "discrete") #> Error: 'modern' is not an exported object from 'namespace:ggrefine'  mtcars |>   count(cyl, am) |>   mutate(     am = if_else(am == 0, "automatic", "manual"),     am = stringr::str_to_sentence(am),     cyl = as.factor(cyl)   ) |>   ggplot(aes(y = am, x = n, colour = cyl, fill = cyl, label = n)) +   geom_col(     position = position_dodge2(preserve = "single", padding = 0.05),     width = ggwidth::get_width(n = 2, n_dodge = 3, orientation = "y"),   ) +   scale_fill_discrete(palette = jumble::jumble) +   scale_colour_discrete(palette = blends::multiply(jumble::jumble)) +   geom_text(     mapping = ggscribe::aes_contrast(), # or aes(!!!ggscribe::aes_contrast()),     position = position_dodge2(       width = ggwidth::get_width(n = 2, n_dodge = 3, orientation = "y"),       preserve = "single",       padding = 0.05,     ),     hjust = 1.25,     show.legend = FALSE,   ) +   scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(c(0, 0.05))) +   ggrefine::modern(y_type = "discrete") #> Error: 'modern' is not an exported object from 'namespace:ggrefine'

title: Annotate an axis bracket — axis_bracket
text: Draws bracket spanning min(breaks) max(breaks) along axis edge floating data position. bar uses rendering path axis_line(); caps use path axis_ticks(). Requires coord_cartesian(clip = "").

title: Annotate an axis bracket — axis_bracket
code: axis_bracket(   ...,   position = NULL,   xintercept = NULL,   yintercept = NULL,   breaks,   colour = NULL,   linewidth = NULL,   linetype = NULL,   ticks_length = ggplot2::rel(1) )

title: Annotate an axis bracket — axis_bracket
text: ... used. Forces named arguments. position One "top", "bottom", "left", "right". Inferred xintercept yintercept provided. xintercept "left"/"right" axes: float bracket x position data coordinates instead panel edge. yintercept "top"/"bottom" axes: float bracket y position data coordinates instead panel edge. breaks numeric vector length >= 2. bar spans min(breaks) max(breaks); caps drawn every break value. colour Inherits axis.ticks set theme (falling back axis.line line). linewidth Inherits axis.ticks set theme. Supports rel(). linetype Inherits axis.ticks set theme. ticks_length Length bracket caps grid unit. Supports rel(). Negative values flip cap direction. Defaults rel(1) (outward theme tick length).

title: Annotate an axis bracket — axis_bracket
text: list ggplot2 annotation layers.

title: Annotate an axis line — axis_line
text: Draws line along axis edge, style defaults taken axis.line element set theme. Requires coord_cartesian(clip = "").

title: Annotate an axis line — axis_line
code: axis_line(   ...,   position = NULL,   xintercept = NULL,   yintercept = NULL,   colour = NULL,   linewidth = NULL,   linetype = NULL )

title: Annotate an axis line — axis_line
text: ... used. Forces named arguments. position One "top", "bottom", "left", "right". Inferred xintercept yintercept provided. xintercept "left"/"right" axes: float axis x position data coordinates instead panel edge. yintercept "top"/"bottom" axes: float axis y position data coordinates instead panel edge. colour Inherits axis.line set theme. linewidth Inherits axis.line set theme. Supports rel(). linetype Inherits axis.line set theme.

title: Annotate an axis line — axis_line
text: list ggplot2 annotation layers.

title: Annotate axis text — axis_text
text: Draws text labels specified break positions along axis, style defaults taken axis.text element set theme. Requires coord_cartesian(clip = "").

title: Annotate axis text — axis_text
code: axis_text(   ...,   position = NULL,   xintercept = NULL,   yintercept = NULL,   breaks,   labels = NULL,   colour = NULL,   size = NULL,   family = NULL,   hjust = NULL,   vjust = NULL,   angle = 0,   ticks_length = ggplot2::rel(1) )

title: Annotate axis text — axis_text
text: ... used. Forces named arguments. position One "top", "bottom", "left", "right". Inferred xintercept yintercept provided. xintercept "left"/"right" axes: float axis x position data coordinates instead panel edge. yintercept "top"/"bottom" axes: float axis y position data coordinates instead panel edge. breaks numeric vector break positions. labels One : NULL (default) use break values labels character vector length breaks function taking break values returning labels colour Inherits axis.text set theme. size Inherits axis.text set theme. family Inherits axis.text set theme. hjust, vjust Justification. Auto-calculated position NULL. angle Text rotation angle. Defaults 0. ticks_length Offset axis edge including tick length margin. Supports rel(). Negative values place labels inside panel. Defaults rel(1) (theme tick length + text margin).

title: Annotate axis text — axis_text
text: list ggplot2 annotation layers.

title: Annotate axis ticks — axis_ticks
text: Draws axis ticks specified break positions, style defaults taken axis.ticks element set theme. Requires coord_cartesian(clip = "").

title: Annotate axis ticks — axis_ticks
code: axis_ticks(   ...,   position = NULL,   xintercept = NULL,   yintercept = NULL,   breaks,   minor = FALSE,   colour = NULL,   linewidth = NULL,   ticks_length = ggplot2::rel(1) )

title: Annotate axis ticks — axis_ticks
text: ... used. Forces named arguments. position One "top", "bottom", "left", "right". Inferred xintercept yintercept provided. xintercept "left"/"right" axes: float axis x position data coordinates instead panel edge. yintercept "top"/"bottom" axes: float axis y position data coordinates instead panel edge. breaks numeric vector break positions. minor Logical. TRUE, uses minor tick theme defaults. Defaults FALSE. colour Inherits axis.ticks set theme. linewidth Inherits axis.ticks set theme. Supports rel(). ticks_length Total tick length grid unit. Supports rel(). Negative values flip tick direction (inward). Defaults rel(1) (outward theme tick length).

title: Annotate axis ticks — axis_ticks
text: list ggplot2 annotation layers.

title: ggscribe: Annotate 'ggplot2' Visualisation — ggscribe-package
text: Annotation helpers 'ggplot2' visualisations. functions make straightforward annotate plots way stays consistent set theme.

title: ggscribe: Annotate 'ggplot2' Visualisation — ggscribe-package
text: Maintainer: David Hodge davidhodge931@gmail.com (ORCID) [copyright holder]

what: Guide for secondary axis annotation — guide_sec_axis
title: Guide for secondary axis annotation — guide_sec_axis
text: wrapper around ggplot2::guide_axis() defaults using theme_sec_axis(). guide designed strip away standard axis furniture (like lines ticks) preserving text, making ideal secondary axes used margin labels.

title: Guide for secondary axis annotation — guide_sec_axis
Guide for secondary axis annotation — guide_sec_axis
text: ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::guide_axis(), title, check.overlap, angle. theme theme object style guide. Defaults theme_sec_axis(), suppresses ticks lines.

title: Guide for secondary axis annotation — guide_sec_axis
text: guide object used scale's guide argument within sec_axis().

title: Guide for secondary axis annotation — guide_sec_axis
code: library(ggplot2)  ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy)) +   ggscribe::reference_line(yintercept = 20) +   geom_point() +   scale_y_continuous(     sec.axis = ggscribe::sec_axis(       breaks = 20,       labels = "Reference",       guide = ggscribe::guide_sec_axis(angle = 270)     )   )

what: Annotate a shaded panel region — panel_shade
title: Annotate a shaded panel region — panel_shade
text: Draws filled rectangle panel colour defaults taken set theme. Defaults subtle overlay across full panel, fill automatically adapting light dark panel backgrounds.

title: Annotate a shaded panel region — panel_shade
code: panel_shade(   ...,   xmin = -Inf,   xmax = Inf,   ymin = -Inf,   ymax = Inf,   fill = "#878580",   alpha = 0.25,   colour = "transparent",   linewidth = NULL,   linetype = NULL )

title: Annotate a shaded panel region — panel_shade
text: ... used. Allows trailing commas named-argument style calls. xmin, xmax Left right edges rectangle. Defaults -Inf Inf. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). ymin, ymax Bottom top edges rectangle. Defaults -Inf Inf. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). fill Fill colour. Defaults neutral grey. alpha Opacity rectangle. Defaults 0.25. colour Border colour. Defaults "transparent". linewidth Inherits panel.border set theme. Supports rel(). linetype Border linetype. Defaults 1.

title: Annotate a shaded panel region — panel_shade
text: list containing annotation layer.

title: Annotate a shaded panel region — panel_shade
code: library(ggplot2)  set_theme(   ggrefine::theme_grey(     panel_heights = rep(unit(50, "mm"), 100),     panel_widths = rep(unit(75, "mm"), 100),    )  )  p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point()  # Shade the full panel p + panel_shade()   # Shade a specific data range p + panel_shade(xmin = 3, xmax = 4)   # Shade using normalized coordinates p + panel_shade(xmin = I(0.25), xmax = I(0.75))   # Custom fill and opacity p + panel_shade(ymin = 20, ymax = 30, fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.15)

title: Annotate a reference line — reference_line
text: Draws reference line within panel, style defaults taken axis.line element set theme. Requires coord_cartesian(clip = "").

title: Annotate a reference line — reference_line
code: reference_line(   ...,   xintercept = NULL,   yintercept = NULL,   colour = NULL,   linewidth = NULL,   linetype = "dashed" )

title: Annotate a reference line — reference_line
text: ... used. Forces named arguments. xintercept Draw vertical reference line x position. yintercept Draw horizontal reference line y position. colour Inherits axis.line set theme. linewidth Inherits axis.line set theme. Supports rel(). linetype Defaults "dashed".

title: Annotate a reference line — reference_line
text: list ggplot2 annotation layers.

what: Duplicate axis with axis text only — sec_axis
title: Duplicate axis with axis text only — sec_axis
text: wrapper around ggplot2::dup_axis() creates secondary axis displaying axis text — axis lines ticks hidden, making useful placing annotation labels (e.g. region labels alongside shaded panel).

title: Duplicate axis with axis text only — sec_axis
code: sec_axis(   breaks = ggplot2::waiver(),   labels = ggplot2::derive(),   name = NULL,   guide = ggplot2::guide_axis(theme = theme_sec_axis()),   ... )

title: Duplicate axis with axis text only — sec_axis
text: breaks One : NULL breaks ggplot2::waiver() (default) inherit breaks primary axis numeric vector break positions function takes scale limits input returns break positions (e.g. \(x) mean(c(x[2], 32))) labels One : ggplot2::derive() (default) derive labels breaks character vector labels, length breaks function takes break positions input returns labels name name secondary axis. Use ggplot2::waiver() derive name primary axis, NULL (default) name. guide guide object used render axis. Defaults guide_sec_axis(), uses theme_sec_axis() make transparent ticks lines default. ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::dup_axis().

title: Duplicate axis with axis text only — sec_axis
text: AxisSecondary object use sec.axis argument scale_x_continuous() scale_y_continuous().

title: Duplicate axis with axis text only — sec_axis
code: library(ggplot2)  ggplot(mpg, aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) +    geom_point() +    panel_shade(ymin = 32) +    scale_y_continuous(      sec.axis = ggscribe::sec_axis(        breaks = \(x) mean(c(x[2], 32)),        labels = "Inefficient",      )    )

what: Theme modifications for secondary axis annotation — theme_sec_axis
title: Theme modifications for secondary axis annotation — theme_sec_axis
text: Theme modifications secondary axis annotation

title: Theme modifications for secondary axis annotation — theme_sec_axis
code: theme_sec_axis(   axis = NULL,   axis_ticks_to = "transparent",   axis_line_to = "transparent",   axis_text_to = "keep",   axis_title_to = "keep" )

title: Theme modifications for secondary axis annotation — theme_sec_axis
text: axis Character. "x", "y", NULL (defaults ). axis_ticks_to Action ticks: "transparent", "blank", "keep". axis_line_to Action lines: "transparent", "blank", "keep". axis_text_to Action text: "transparent", "blank", "keep". axis_title_to Action titles: "transparent", "blank", "keep".

title: Theme modifications for secondary axis annotation — theme_sec_axis
text: ggplot2 theme object.
